zh.Villnäs, Lokalks, Turku pori, Finland: 芬蘭圖爾庫 亞 波里盧薩阿利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärd於大約1371年出生在芬蘭圖爾庫 亞 波里盧薩阿利時，他的父親Herman Svärd42歲，母親Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurki32歲。他和Cecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lama至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1428年5月18日在芬蘭享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärd于大约1371年出生在芬兰圖爾庫 亞 波里盧薩阿利时，他的父亲Herman Svärd42岁，母亲Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurki32岁。他和Cecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lama至少有1个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1428年5月18日在芬兰享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hermansson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Helmer, Anders, Bjorn, Per.
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Hermansson: patronymic from the personal name Herman .
Americanized form of Danish and Norwegian Hermansen , a cognate of 1 above, and of its rare variant Hermannsen, which is also North German.

variants.name: Herman
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Dutch, Flemish, Hungarian, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian, and Croatian: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. Compare Harman 5.
Americanized form of German   Hermann  , a cognate of 1 above.
English: perhaps a shortened form of Herniman, a topographic name for a ‘dweller (in a) nook or corner of land’, from Middle English herne, hirne, hurne + man. See also Hearn .

nameMeanings.name: Kurki
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Czech: nickname for someone thought to resemble a hen, from a diminutive of kura ‘hen, domestic fowl’.

nameMeanings.name: till
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Till, a pet form of Old French Matild(e) (in its Latin form Matilda), which was an alternative to Maud; see Maude 2. A diminutive form occurs in Tillotson .
North German: variant of   Thiel  .

variants.name: Tilly
meanings: German: from a shortened form of Tilling, which itself is a patronymic from Till .
English and Irish: variant of   Tilley  .
Irish: variant of   Tully  .

variants.name: Tyl
meanings: Polish and Czech: from the personal name Tyl, a Slavicized form of German Till and Thiel 1. The same Sorbian surname, which may also be from a short form of the personal name Bartyl ‘Bartholomew’ (see Barthel ), is found only in a Germanized form, Thiel 2.

variants.name: Maude
meanings: English (of Norman origin):

habitational name from Mold in Flintshire, Wales, which name derives from Anglo-Norman French mont haut ‘high hill’ (Latinised as mons altus), the Norman name for Bailey Hill on which the castle stood. The name de Montalt, de Mohaut was borne by an Anglo-Norman aristocratic family who acted as castle wardens there but who had their chief seat in Riddlesden in Yorkshire, where the surname proliferated as Mawhood and Maude. In the 17th century one branch of the family acquired lands in Ireland. The Maude baronetcy of Dundrum, Tipperary, was created in 1705 for Robert Maude. His son Cornwallis Maude, Baron de Montalt, was created first Viscount Hawarden in 1793. Mowatt is the Scottish form of the name, from a different branch of the same family.

occasionally from Middle English and Norman French female personal name Mahald or Maud, which are Old French forms of ancient Germanic Mahthild (Latinised as Matilda), composed of the Germanic elements maht ‘might, power’ + hild ‘battle’. This personal name partly owed its great popularity among Normans in England to the fact that it was the name of the wife (c. 1031–83) of William the Conqueror. Surnames derived from this personal name are found in many forms and spellings, for example, Mould and Moll .

variants.name: Tillmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ruedi, Franz, Georg, Helmut, Joerg.  Scandinavian Holger, Johan.
North German: elaborated form of Till , with the addition of -mann ‘man’.

variants.name: Tillett
meanings: English (East Anglia): from the Middle English female personal name Tillot, a diminutive of Till , itself a pet form of Matill (Matilda). Compare Tillotson .

variants.name: Tillis
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Tills, itself a variant of Till , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Tilles  .

nameMeanings.name: Villnäs
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Nestor, Alejandro, Carmelita, Jorge, Julio, Luz, Manuel, Orlando, Rafaela, Roque.

Spanish: habitational name from a place called Villas in Cáceres (Extremadura) or Las Villas in northern Spain, named with the plural of villa ‘(outlying) farmstead, (dependent) settlement’ (see Villa ).

variants.name: Villa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Jesus, Carlos, Luis, Mario, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ramon.  Italian Antonio, Lorenzo, Angelo, Dario, Federico, Luciano, Dante, Leonardo, Marco, Mauro, Salvatore, Vito.

Spanish: topographic name from villa ‘(outlying) farmstead, (dependent) settlement’ (see 2 below), or a habitational name from any of the places (mainly in Asturias) called Villa, or from any of numerous places called with this as the first element. Compare Billa and Lavilla .

Italian: topographic name for someone who lived in a village as opposed to an isolated farmhouse, or in a town as opposed to the countryside, from Latin villa ‘country house, estate’, a term later used to denote a group of houses forming a settlement and in some dialects to denote the most important area or center of a settlement. This may also be a habitational name from any of various places called with villa. Compare Davilla .

nameMeanings.name: Svärd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Henri, Jean-Claude, Remy.
French and Swiss French: shortened form of   Evrard  .

variants.name: Evrard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Emile, Herard, Jean Claude, Jean-Marc, Jeanmarie, Marcel.
French and Walloon: from the ancient Germanic personal name Eberhard (see Eberhardt ).
English: variant of   Everett  .

nameMeanings.name: Nils
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of   Niklas .

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nels
meanings: vernacular form of   Niklas .

en.lifeSummary: When Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärd was born about 1371, in Louhisaari, Turku ja Pori, Finland, his father, Herman Svärd, was 42 and his mother, Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurki, was 32. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Cecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lama. He died on 18 May 1428, in Finland, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärd est né environ 1371, à Louhisaari, Turku ja Pori, Finlande, son père, Herman Svärd, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurki, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec Cecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lama. Il est décédé le 18 mai 1428, à Finlande, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärd um 1371 in Louhisaari, Turku ja Pori, Finnland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Herman Svärd 42 und seine Mutter Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurki 32. Er hatte mit Cecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lama mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 18. Mai 1428 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Finnland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärd è nato il circa 1371 nel Louhisaari, Turku ja Pori, Finlandia, il suo padre, Herman Svärd, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurki, aveva 32 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con Cecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lama. Morì il 18 maggio 1428, nel Finlandia, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärdが約1371年にフィンランドトゥルク ジャ ポリロウヒサアリで生まれた時，父Herman Svärdは42歳，母Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurkiは32歳であった。彼はCecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lamaとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1428年5月18日にフィンランドで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärd(이)가 대략 1371년 핀란드 터르쿠 자 포리 루히사아리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herman Svärd(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurki(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Cecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lama(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1428년 5월 18일 핀란드에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärd nasceu aproximadamente 1371, em Louhisaari, Turku ja Pori, Finlândia, seu pai, Herman Svärd, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurki, tinha 32 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas com Cecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lama. Ele faleceu em 18 de maio de 1428, em Finlândia, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärd родился около 1371 г. в местности лоухисаари, турку джа пори, Финляндия, его отцу по имени Herman Svärd было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurki, 32. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Cecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lama. Он умер 18 мая 1428 г. в местности Финляндия в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nils Hermansson Kurki till Villnäs Svärd nació aproximadamente 1371, en Louhisaari, Turku ja Pori, Finlandia, su papá, Herman Svärd, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Birgitta Jacobsdotter Kurki, tenía 32 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas con Cecilia Filipsdotter Folkunga Lama. Murió el 18 de mayo de 1428, en Finlandia, a la edad de 58 años.
